brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: Let me show you what a join does first. Then we'll use it.

template.text: timestamp, message, tags['service'] as service, team

expected.sql: SELECT timestamp, message, logs.tags['service'] as service, team FROM logs JOIN services ON logs.tags['service'] = service_name LIMIT 10

hints.text: Carol: 'Query error. Connect the two tables with tags.service = service_name.'
Carol: 'Zero rows. The join condition connects tag values to catalog columns — try tags.service = service_name.'
Carol: 'Too many rows. LIMIT 10 is already there.'
Carol: 'tags.service = service_name — the service tag matches the catalog column.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text: Look at that. Every log row now has a team attached. That's what a join does — it reaches across tables and staples the context on.
